title: samolus valerandi subsp. parviflorus
intro: Samolus valerandi subsp. parviflorus, commonly known as dwarf marsh St. John's wort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the primrose family that is native to Europe and western Asia. The medicinal properties of Samolus valerandi subsp. parviflorus include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Samolus valerandi subsp. parviflorus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal uses.
